items.title: Tezos Tests Post-Quantum Privacy as Founder Slams 'Half-Baked' Bitcoin Quantum Theories
items.summary: Arthur Breitman's comments come as Tezos tests a post-quantum privacy system designed to protect encrypted blockchain data from future attacks.

items.title: Turnkey raises $12.5 million in round backed by Circle Ventures and Sequoia Capital
items.summary: The new capital will primarily fund the development and public launch of Turnkey Verifiable Cloud, a secure computing product for digital assets.

items.title: Stablecoin-powered neobank Fasset raises $51 million to expand across emerging markets
items.summary: The Shariah-compliant digital bank is part of a growing wave of fintech startups building banking and payments services on top of blockchain and stablecoin rails.

items.title: BlackRock, Janus Henderson tokenized funds get instant redemptions with new $1 billion facility
items.summary: A new credit facility by Grove aims to allow instant redemptions into stablecoins from BlackRock's BUIDL and Janus Henderson's money market funds, reducing settlement time from days to instantaneous.

items.title: Bitcoin’s Dip Below $80K Could Be ‘Short-Lived’ as STRC Cycle Looms
items.summary: Strategy's STRC stock has fueled mid-month Bitcoin rallies since March, but May demand is plateauing as ETFs posted $630 million outflows.

items.title: Coinbase backs Hyperliquid stablecoin push as DeFi trading volumes climb
items.summary: Coinbase will manage USDC liquidity on Hyperliquid, deepening ties with one of crypto’s fastest-growing trading platforms.

items.title: Strive’s SATA to become first U.S. listed security to pay daily cash dividends
items.summary: The daily payout structure lifts the effective yield to 13.88% as the company eliminates all debt and expands its bitcoin treasury strategy.

items.title: Morning Minute: What’s At Stake with Trump in Beijing
items.summary: Trump's in Beijing for the biggest potential market-moving event of the year, while Claude helped one lucky user recover $400,000 in Bitcoin.

items.title: Bank of England ready to water down 'overly conservative' stablecoin proposals: FT
items.summary: Restrictions proposed by the BOE prompted criticism from the crypto industry, which said they risked preventing the U.K. from being competitive in the digital economy.

items.title: Bitcoin’s recent $80,000 breakout was led by something other than U.S. spot buyers, data show
items.summary: The rally was led by leveraged traders and not U.S.-based spot buyers. Hence, its. sustainability is being questioned.

items.title: Law firm Fenwick & West sued for $525M over alleged role in FTX collapse
items.summary: Twenty FTX victims are suing Fenwick & West, claiming the law firm didn’t just represent FTX, it helped build the infrastructure that kept the fraud running.

items.title: JPMorgan lifts Bitcoin ETF exposure in Q1, led by BlackRock’s IBIT

items.title: Bullish misses first-quarter revenue estimates on weaker crypto trading activity
items.summary: The company also missed bottom-line forecasts; shares were down nearly 8% in pre-market trading.

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs Shed $630M in Largest Daily Exit Since January
items.summary: Inflation fears and Fed uncertainty trigger the biggest U.S. Bitcoin ETF outflows since January, snapping weeks of institutional inflows.

items.title: Bitcoin stuck below $80,000 as leveraged longs unwind, altcoins slide

items.title: Altcoin season ‘quietly’ starting? Analysts spot three bullish indicators

items.title: BitGo posts wider Q1 loss despite revenue more than doubling
